name: Waffles
description: Crispy, light, and delicious old-fashioned waffles adapted from Great Grandmother Catharine's recipe.
instructions: Sift flour with baking powder, salt, and sugar.
Separate egg whites and egg yolks into two bowls.
Make a hole in the center of the sifted ingredients.
Pour in the egg yolks, butter, and milk.
Combine with a few swift strokes until batter is pebbled looking.
Beat egg whites until stiff, but not dry.
Fold egg whites into batter until they are barely blended.
Spray waffle iron with cooking spray and pre-heat.
Pour 1.5 cups of batter onto waffle iron and spread with a spatula until bottom surface is covered.
Close the lid and cook for 4 minutes, or until waffle iron sounds as done and steam has stopped emerging.
Serve with maple syrup, honey butter, and/or sweetened strawberries.
